content.claim: Hungary exhibits the highest level of student segregation by socioeconomic advantage among EU/OECD countries (2022 PISA), and pupils in segregated schools are less likely to complete upper secondary education or enter tertiary education.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Causal attribution (whether 6-/8-year selection structures *cause* segregation or channel pre-existing sorting demand) is contested and not resolved in available literature
PISA data does not isolate the 6-/8-year track mechanism from other selection pathways (school choice, residential sorting, 4-year gimnázium intake)
Segregation outcome studies do not provide direct cost-benefit comparison with alternative track structures
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
